lesson1.title: The Breakfast Robot
lesson1.story: Meet Bleep! He needs help making toast. Help him by putting the steps in order!
lesson1.success: Great job! You made an Algorithm!
lesson1.retry: Oops! Bleep made a mess. Try a different order!

lesson2.title: The Weather Coder
lesson2.story: Help Bleep decide what to wear! Look at the sky and complete the code.
lesson2.error_wrong_condition: Look at the sky! Is it really {weather}?
lesson2.error_wrong_action: Does {weather} mean you need {action}? Think again!

lesson3.title: The Robot Dance-Off
lesson3.story: Bleep wants to win the dance contest! Instead of writing the same move over and over, use a LOOP to make him repeat his favourite dance steps.
lesson3.instruction: Make Bleep SPIN 4 times using only ONE move block.
lesson3.success: Woohoo! You're a Loop Legend! Your code is now much shorter.
lesson3.hint: Put the 'Spin Around' block INSIDE the 'Repeat' block.
lesson3.feedback_too_verbose: Phew, that's a lot of work! Can you make it shorter with a Loop?
lesson3.feedback_need_spin: Bleep needs to SPIN! Try the Spin Around block.
lesson3.feedback_wrong_count: Bleep needs to spin {count} times! Change the number.

lesson4.title: The Magic Boxes
lesson4.story: Bleep needs to count how many Pastéis de Nata he collects! Use a 'Variable' box to store the number.
lesson4.instruction: Click each Pastel de Nata to collect it. Fill the variable to 5!
lesson4.success: Amazing! You used a Variable to count to 5!
lesson4.hint: Click on a Pastel de Nata to collect it!

lesson5.title: The Adventure Backpack
lesson5.story: Bleep is going to the beach! Help him put a hat and sunscreen into his 'List' (Array).
lesson5.instruction: Drag the 3 beach items into the backpack. Don't pack the trash!
lesson5.trash_feedback: That doesn't belong in a backpack!
lesson5.success: Packed and ready! You used an Array to store items!
lesson5.hint: Drag the hat, sunscreen and towel — skip the trash!

lesson6.title: The Super Move
lesson6.story: Why repeat 3 blocks when you can make one 'Function'? Name your custom move 'Mega-Jump'.
lesson6.instruction: Build your Mega-Jump function, then CALL it!
lesson6.success: Incredible! You defined AND called a Function!
lesson6.feedback_empty: The function is empty! Give me some moves first!
lesson6.feedback_missing_clap: Mega-Jump needs a Clap block too!
lesson6.hint: Put Jump, Spin and Clap inside the function, then call it!

lesson7.title: The Secret Listener
lesson7.story: Bleep is asleep! He only wakes up if you click his nose or press 'Space'. Connect the keys to his moves.
lesson7.instruction: Drag a move block into the ON SPACE PRESSED box, then press the button to test it!
lesson7.success: Amazing! You created an Event Listener!
lesson7.feedback_empty: Add a move block first, then press Space!
lesson7.feedback_wrong: Bleep reacts — but to WIN, make him Jump!
lesson7.hint: Put the Jump 🦘 block inside the ON SPACE PRESSED box, then press the button!

lesson8.title: The Bug Detective
lesson8.story: Oh no! Bleep's code is broken. He's walking backwards! Find the wrong block and fix it.
lesson8.instruction: Click the wrong block to remove it, then click the right one from the tray below!
lesson8.success: Bug squashed! You're a Bug Detective! 🔍
lesson8.feedback_wrong: Oops! Bleep went the wrong way. Can you spot the bug?
lesson8.hint: Block 3 is wrong! 'Move Left' sends Bleep backwards — replace it with 'Move Right'!

lesson9.title: The Hidden Castle
lesson9.story: Use X and Y numbers to find the castle. Remember: X is side-to-side, Y is up-and-down!
lesson9.instruction: Set X to 8 and Y to 3 — that's where the castle is hiding!
lesson9.success: You found the castle! Coordinates expert! 🗺️
lesson9.feedback_miss: You landed at (X:{x}, Y:{y}) but the castle is at (X:8, Y:3). Try again!
lesson9.hint: The castle is hiding at X: 8, Y: 3. Use the + and – buttons to adjust!

lesson10.title: The Star Catcher
lesson10.story: Combine everything! If Bleep touches a star, your 'Score' variable goes up. Catch 10 to graduate!
lesson10.instruction: Move Bleep left and right to catch the falling stars. Catch 10 to win!
lesson10.success: Incredible! You've mastered the basics. You are now a Junior Programmer! Ready for Phase 2?
lesson10.phase1_sub: Junior Programmer — The Basics are yours!
lesson10.hint: Move Bleep under the falling ⭐ stars to catch them. The code loop runs automatically!

lesson11.title: Secret Messages
lesson11.mission: Bleep found an old radio! Help him join words together to send a secret message.
lesson11.story: Coding isn't just numbers. We also use 'Strings' (Text). Let's join two words together!
lesson11.success: Amazing! You just performed your first text 'Concatenation'! 📻✨
lesson11.hint: Drag 'Hello' into the first slot and 'Bleep' into the second slot, then press Run!

lesson12.title: The Digital Library
lesson12.story: Bleep wants to know if it's raining in Lisbon! But he can't look outside — he needs to ASK the internet.
lesson12.goal: Drag the fetchData block into the script to call the API!
lesson12.success: Incredible! You just fetched data from an API!
lesson12.hint: Drag the fetchData block into the script box, then run it!

lesson13.title: The Gatekeeper
lesson13.story: Only robots with a valid ID can enter the castle. An ID is valid if it is exactly 4 characters long.
lesson13.goal: Use the Validation rule to check which robot can enter!
lesson13.success: Well done! You used Validation to protect the castle!
lesson13.hint: Only the robot with exactly 4 characters in their ID is allowed in!

lesson14.title: The Moon Jump
lesson14.story: Bleep is on the Moon! Gravity is weak here. Can you change the 'Gravity' variable so Bleep can jump over the giant crater?
lesson14.goal: Set Gravity to '2' to jump high, or '10' to stay on the ground.
lesson14.clear_feedback: Amazing! Low gravity = high jump! Bleep cleared the crater!
lesson14.fail_feedback: Too much gravity! Bleep fell in. Lower the gravity and try again!
lesson14.success: Incredible! You understand how a Constant changes the whole world!
lesson14.hint: Set gravity to 4 or lower, then press Jump!

lesson15.title: The Shield Generator
lesson15.mission: 10 meteors are heading for Bleep's planet! Build a wall of shields to stop them!
lesson15.goal: Drag both blocks into the loop — Bleep will place a shield, then step right!
lesson15.success: Amazing! You built a defence wall using a loop! Every iteration placed one shield and stepped right. 🛡️🚀
lesson15.hint: Drag 'clone(shield)' into the first slot, then 'move_right(40)' into the second slot.

lesson16.title: The Turbo Booster
lesson16.story: Bleep's rocket is stuck on the launch pad! Set the X and Y velocity to blast it to the space portal!
lesson16.goal: Set BOTH velocities and hit the 🌀 portal to win!
lesson16.hint_diagonal: Give the rocket power in BOTH X and Y directions!
lesson16.success: 🚀 You mastered 2D Velocity! The rocket found the portal!
lesson16.hint: Set both sliders to 7 or higher, then Ignite!

lesson17.title: The Infinite Battery
lesson17.story: Bleep's battery is low! A single charge() only adds 1%. Use a 'While' loop to keep charging until it reaches 100%.
lesson17.goal: Drag the charge() block into the while loop, then press Run!
lesson17.mission_hint: Hint: Build the 'battery < 100' condition to fully charge!
lesson17.success: 🔋 Battery full! You mastered the While Loop!
lesson17.hint: Drag battery → op (<) → 100 into the condition, then charge() into the loop body!
lesson17.tired_feedback: Only 1%! Bleep is exhausted 😴  Use the while loop to keep going!

lesson18.title: The Security Guard
lesson18.story: To open the vault, Bleep needs the Secret Key AND the correct Fingerprint. One alone is never enough!
lesson18.goal: Place both condition blocks, turn both scanners ON, then press Run.
lesson18.success: 🔐 Vault open! You mastered Logical AND!
lesson18.denied: ❌ Access Denied! Both conditions must be TRUE.
lesson18.hint: Place hasKey in slot 1 and hasFingerprint in slot 2. Turn BOTH scanners ON, then press Run!

stickerBook.title: My Badge Collection
stickerBook.subtitle: Collect all 20 badges to become a Master Architect!
stickerBook.no_badges: No badges yet — go complete a lesson!

chef_logic.name: Master Chef
chef_logic.description: You put steps in the right order — just like a real recipe! Sequences are the building blocks of every program.

weather_wizard.name: Weather Wizard
weather_wizard.description: You used IF and THEN to make smart decisions! Conditionals let your code react to the world.

loop_dancer.name: Loop Dancer
loop_dancer.description: Instead of repeating yourself, you used a LOOP — that's what smart coders do!

magic_box.name: Magic Box
magic_box.description: You stored a number in a VARIABLE — now your code can remember things!

backpack_pro.name: Adventure Hiker
backpack_pro.description: You packed items into an ARRAY — like a shopping list your code can read!

func_hero.name: Function Hero
func_hero.description: You defined AND called a FUNCTION — the secret power of all great programmers!

click_master.name: Secret Listener
click_master.description: You learned about EVENTS — your code can listen and react to clicks, keys, and more!

bug_detective.name: Bug Detective
bug_detective.description: You found and fixed a bug — every great programmer is also a great detective!

map_explorer.name: Map Explorer
map_explorer.description: You navigated with X and Y coordinates — now you can place anything, anywhere on screen!

game_master.name: Game Master
game_master.description: You handled COLLISIONS — the logic that makes games feel real!

secret_coder.name: Secret Messenger
secret_coder.description: You worked with STRINGS — text is data too, and you know how to use it!

data_librarian.name: Data Librarian
data_librarian.description: You fetched data from an API — your code can now talk to the whole internet!

castle_guard.name: Castle Guard
castle_guard.description: You validated data — protecting your program from bad input like a true guardian!

moon_jumper.name: Moon Jumper
moon_jumper.description: You simulated gravity and physics — making objects move just like on the Moon!

clone_commander.name: Clone Commander
clone_commander.description: You cloned objects — one piece of code, infinite copies. Very efficient!

race_driver.name: Estoril Racer
race_driver.description: You built acceleration logic — speed, friction, and momentum all in your code!

ai_trainer.name: While Loop Wizard
ai_trainer.description: You used a WHILE loop to keep charging until the battery hit 100% — loops that run until a condition is met!

logic_path.name: Vault Guardian
logic_path.description: You combined two conditions with AND — both must be true at the same time to open the vault!

world_builder.name: World Builder
world_builder.description: You designed a level from scratch — part programmer, part architect, all awesome!

master_architect.name: Master Architect
master_architect.description: You completed ALL 20 lessons. You are a true coding master. The journey begins here!

profile.setupSub: One last step before you start coding.
profile.parentEmailHelp: We'll send them a quick email to say you're ready to code!

waitingRoom.title: Almost there! ⏳
waitingRoom.message: We sent an email to your parents! Ask them to click the link so you can start coding.
waitingRoom.subMessage: Once they say yes, come back and click a lesson!

ageGate.heading: Privacy & Protection
ageGate.body: We need to verify your age before you create an account. If you are under 16 years of age, your parent or guardian will be asked to give their consent on the next page. We do not share your data with third parties.
ageGate.checkboxLabel: I confirm I am over 16 years of age. If you are under 16 years of age, you will be asked to provide parental consent on the next page.
ageGate.ageVerifiedNote: You confirmed you are 16 or older — no parental consent needed!

auth.signInSub: Sign in to continue your coding journey.
auth.signUpSub: Create an account to start learning.
auth.lockedSub: Sign in or create a free account to play.
